title: v5.0.0 release
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *lsm5* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v5.0/OWNERS)~~ [lsm5] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Cockpit tests failed for commit f32338dfce2b0a2d48724ce96602331d21ab3aa2. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

LGTM
cokpit-revdeps is not happy though.

> cokpit-revdeps is not happy though.

yeah rawhide is broken at the moment (https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22052) but this is outside of our control so it doesn't block the release. f39/40 work so we are good.
@Luap99 is correct. LGTM from my end, but I'll leave it to @baude to push the button when he is happy about image creation.
Waiting until after lunch so we can get image builds going at the same time

> > cokpit-revdeps is not happy though.
> 
> yeah rawhide is broken at the moment (#22052) but this is outside of our control so it doesn't block the release. f39/40 work so we are good.

Update: With the latest [podman-5.0.0~rc7-1.fc40 ](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2024-9c9174f3dd) and [passt-0^20240319.gd35bcbe-1.fc40](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2024-3deab8ca81), user podman now also fails on F40. These aren't "visible" to dnf in normal runs yet, but I pulled them directly from koji in https://github.com/cockpit-project/cockpit-podman/pull/1636 . So you may want to hold off on these?

> > > cokpit-revdeps is not happy though.
> > 
> > 
> > yeah rawhide is broken at the moment (#22052) but this is outside of our control so it doesn't block the release. f39/40 work so we are good.
> 
> Update: With the latest [podman-5.0.0~rc7-1.fc40 ](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2024-9c9174f3dd) and [passt-0^20240319.gd35bcbe-1.fc40](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2024-3deab8ca81), user podman now also fails on F40. These aren't "visible" to dnf in normal runs yet, but I pulled them directly from koji in [cockpit-project/cockpit-podman#1636](https://github.com/cockpit-project/cockpit-podman/pull/1636) . So you may want to hold off on these?

I don't think we should block on this stuff because AFAICT these are pasta bugs and must be fixed there. It is unfortunate timing for sure but we have a lot of other things that we like to get out sooner than later.
